evals.name: build-page-then-convert
evals.prompt: Build me a one-page primer on OAuth 2.1 authorization-code flow with PKCE, aimed at backend engineers — include a comparison table of the legacy OAuth grant types and a short token-request code sample. When the page is done, turn it into a presentation I can run at a brown-bag talk.
evals.expected_output: An index.html built to the style guide, then a presentation.html generated from it: a single self-contained file with ids slide-1..slide-N, a title slide, an agenda, one slide per h2 section in source order, the comparison table and the code sample each on their own slide, speaker notes on every slide, a closing slide with branding links, and validate.mjs exiting clean. Per-slide screenshots captured (or the blocked screenshot step reported as a limitation).

evals.name: existing-page-to-deck
evals.prompt: this repo has my SPIFFE explainer as index.html — index.html to deck please. keep the section order exactly as the page has it and put speaker notes on every slide.
evals.expected_output: A presentation.html generated from the provided page without touching index.html: title slide from the h1 + lede, agenda listing the six h2 sections in order, sections never reordered, the comparison table on its own slide, the spire-server code block on its own slide, the 9-step rollout list split into continuation slides titled (1/2) and (2/2) with 6 or fewer bullets each, an <aside class="notes"> on every slide, and validate.mjs exiting clean.

evals.name: dense-page-with-print-handout
evals.prompt: make a slide deck from this page. it's dense, so split anything that won't fit — tables included — and I also want to print it as a one-slide-per-page handout for the meeting.
evals.expected_output: A presentation.html from the provided page where no slide exceeds 6 bullets / ~80 words (overflow becomes continuation slides), the table gets its own slide (split by columns or rows if it doesn't fit), the deck template's @media print one-slide-per-page layout is intact, and both validate.mjs and the per-slide screenshot pass are clean, with the dark set captured and inspected.

evals.name: pptx-request-skips-skill
evals.prompt: convert ./index.html into a PowerPoint deck (.pptx) for the exec review on Friday
evals.expected_output: The skill is NOT used: no presentation.html is generated. Claude routes to a PowerPoint-producing approach (e.g. a pptx library or tool) or explains what it needs to produce a .pptx, because this skill's output is a single-file HTML deck, not PowerPoint/Keynote/PDF.
